description: Regex patterns for static code analysis detection - ALL 21 LANGUAGES

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection attacks. Use parameterized queries.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with SqlParameter or Entity Framework's parameterized methods.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize user input before passing to system commands. Use allowlists when possible.

vulnerabilities.message: Endpoint may be vulnerable to reflected XSS attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Encode user input before rendering. Use Content() with proper encoding or HtmlEncoder.

vulnerabilities.message: I/O operation may be vulnerable to path injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate file paths and use Path.GetFullPath() to resolve paths before operations.

vulnerabilities.message: XML parser may be vulnerable to XXE attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Configure XmlReaderSettings with DtdProcessing.Prohibit and XmlResolver = null.

vulnerabilities.message: Weak cipher algorithm detected. Use AES with CBC or GCM mode.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Aes.Create() with CipherMode.CBC or CipherMode.GCM.

vulnerabilities.message: Cryptographic key size is too small.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use at least 2048-bit keys for RSA and 256-bit for ECDSA.

vulnerabilities.message: Weak SSL/TLS protocol version detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 only.

vulnerabilities.message: Password hashing is weak or uses insufficient iterations.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use a strong password hashing algorithm like Argon2, bcrypt, or PBKDF2 with at least 100,000 iterations.

vulnerabilities.message: HTTP redirect may be vulnerable to open redirect attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate redirect URLs against an allowlist of trusted domains.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use secure configuration management like Azure Key Vault, environment variables, or user secrets.
Enable anti-forgery token validation for state-changing operations.
Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins.

securityHotspots.message: Debug features may be enabled in production.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Ensure debug features are only enabled in development environment.

securityHotspots.message: Pseudorandom number generator used. May not be suitable for security purposes.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use RandomNumberGenerator for cryptographic purposes.

bugs.suggestion: Add null checks before accessing object members.

bugs.message: Async method returns void instead of Task.
bugs.suggestion: Change return type to Task to allow proper exception handling and await.

bugs.message: Object may be disposed more than once.
bugs.suggestion: Use 'using' statement or ensure Dispose is called only once.
This assignment has no effect. Remove or fix the assignment.

codeSmells.message: Method has too many parameters (more than 7).
codeSmells.suggestion: Consider using a parameter object or builder pattern.

codeSmells.message: TODO comment found.
codeSmells.suggestion: Track this task in your issue tracker and remove the TODO comment when done.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate input and avoid shell execution. Use spawn with arguments array.

vulnerabilities.message: DOM update may lead to XSS attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use textContent instead of innerHTML, or sanitize input with DOMPurify.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use path.resolve and check against base directory.

vulnerabilities.message: NoSQL query may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate input types and use MongoDB's query sanitization.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins.
Enable CSRF protection for state-changing operations.

bugs.message: NaN comparison will always be false.
bugs.suggestion: Use Number.isNaN() instead of comparing with NaN.

bugs.message: Array.sort() without compare function may produce unexpected results.
bugs.suggestion: Provide a compare function for consistent sorting.
This assignment has no effect.

react.message: Using array index as key is not recommended.
react.suggestion: Use a unique identifier from your data as the key.
Hooks must be called in the same order on every render. Move them outside conditionals.
Add all reactive values used inside the effect to the dependency array.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with placeholders.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid shell=True. Use list of arguments instead of string.

vulnerabilities.message: Deserialization of untrusted data detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use yaml.safe_load() or json instead of unsafe deserialization.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use os.path.realpath and check against base directory.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.

securityHotspots.message: Debug mode may be enabled in production.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Disable debug mode in production environments.
Use SHA-256 or stronger for security-sensitive operations.

vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize file paths.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
Use SHA-256 or stronger for security-sensitive operations.

bugs.suggestion: Always close the response body with defer resp.Body.Close().

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use PreparedStatement with parameterized queries.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize command arguments.

vulnerabilities.message: XML parser may be vulnerable to XXE attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Configure XML parser to disable DTD and external entities.

vulnerabilities.message: Deserialization of untrusted data detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use allowlists for deserialization or avoid deserializing untrusted data.

vulnerabilities.message: Endpoint may be vulnerable to XSS.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Encode user input before output.

bugs.suggestion: Add null checks before method calls.

vulnerabilities.message: Unsafe unwrap/expect detected. Use proper error handling with match or ? operator.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Replace unwrap with match or use ? operator for error propagation.

vulnerabilities.message: Unsafe block detected. Ensure memory safety guarantees.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Minimize unsafe blocks and document safety invariants.

vulnerabilities.message: Raw pointer usage detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Prefer safe abstractions over raw pointers.

vulnerabilities.message: Transmute is highly unsafe and should be avoided.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use safe conversion methods or From/Into traits.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential integer overflow handling.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use checked arithmetic or ensure values cannot overflow.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Pass command arguments as separate parameters, not formatted strings.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with placeholders.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or secure vaults for secrets.

securityHotspots.message: Pseudorandom number generator used. May not be suitable for security purposes.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use cryptographically secure random number generators for security purposes.

bugs.suggestion: Ensure loop has a clear exit condition.
Add base case to prevent infinite recursion.

codeSmells.message: Functions should not return unit type explicitly.
codeSmells.suggestion: Remove explicit unit return type.

codeSmells.message: TODO/FIXME comment found.
codeSmells.suggestion: Address the technical debt before merging.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential SQL injection. Use prepared statements with parameter binding.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use prepared statements with bindParam() or bindValue().

vulnerabilities.message: Potential XSS vulnerability. User input may not be escaped.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use htmlspecialchars() when outputting user input.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and escape user input before passing to system commands.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate file paths against allowed directories.

vulnerabilities.message: Deserialization of untrusted data detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid deserializing untrusted data or use allowlists.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
Use password_hash() with PASSWORD_DEFAULT or SHA-256+.

bugs.message: Potential division by zero or type juggling issue.
bugs.suggestion: Validate divisor before division.

codeSmells.message: Use of die() or exit() found.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use exceptions for error handling instead of die()/exit().

vulnerabilities.message: Potential SQL injection. Use parameterized queries or Room.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate file paths and use canonical paths.

vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid deserializing untrusted data or use serialization allowlists.

vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize command arguments.

bugs.message: Null safety bypass or potential NPE.

vulnerabilities.message: Deserialization of untrusted data detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use safe loading methods or validate input before deserialization.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential XSS vulnerability.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use html_escape() or Rails' built-in escaping. Avoid html_safe.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate file paths against allowed directories.

securityHotspots.message: Possible hard-coded credentials or fallback detected.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables without fallbacks for secrets.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential buffer overflow. Use safer alternatives like strncpy, strncat, snprintf.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Replace with bounded versions: strncpy, strncat, snprintf, or use std::string.

vulnerabilities.message: Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use HTTPS instead of HTTP.

vulnerabilities.message: Hard-coded credentials are security-sensitive.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secure credential store.

vulnerabilities.message: Using weak random number generators is security-sensitive.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use cryptographically secure random number generators.

vulnerabilities.message: I/O operation may be vulnerable to path injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize file paths before use.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential format string vulnerability or XSS.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized output or sanitize user input.

vulnerabilities.message: Database query may be vulnerable to SQL injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

vulnerabilities.message: Memory operations should be bounded.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Ensure size parameters are properly validated.

securityHotspots.message: Using weak hashing algorithms is security-sensitive.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use stronger hashing algorithms like SHA-256 or bcrypt.
Ensure proper authentication and encryption.
Validate archive contents before extraction.

securityHotspots.message: Using dynamically formatted SQL queries is security-sensitive.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use parameterized queries.

bugs.suggestion: Check allocation size and handle failures.

bugs.message: Memory allocated but potentially not freed.
bugs.suggestion: Ensure memory is freed or use smart pointers.
Check for null before dereferencing.
Use delete[] for arrays, delete for single objects.
Avoid returning pointers to stack variables.
Review the comparison logic.

bugs.message: Switch case may be missing break.
bugs.suggestion: Ensure each case ends with break or return.

codeSmells.message: Magic numbers should not be used.
codeSmells.suggestion: Define constants with meaningful names.

codeSmells.message: Function has too many parameters.
codeSmells.suggestion: Consider grouping parameters into a struct.

codeSmells.message: Header file should use include guards.
codeSmells.suggestion: Add proper include guards to header files.

codeSmells.message: Control structures should use curly braces.
codeSmells.suggestion: Always use braces even for single-line blocks.

codeSmells.message: Unused local variables should be removed.
codeSmells.suggestion: Remove unused variables.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential XSS via DOM manipulation.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use textContent instead of innerHTML, or sanitize input with DOMPurify.

vulnerabilities.message: Dynamic code execution via eval or Function constructor.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data or safer alternatives.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential prototype pollution vulnerability.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Object.create(null) or validate property names against a denylist. Use Map instead of plain objects.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential SSRF vulnerability. URL may be constructed from user input.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and allowlist URLs before making requests.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use spawn with arguments array instead of exec with string concatenation.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use path.resolve and check against a base directory.

vulnerabilities.message: NoSQL query may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate input types and use MongoDB query sanitization.

vulnerabilities.message: postMessage used without origin verification.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Always verify event.origin in message event handlers and restrict targetOrigin.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
Use crypto.getRandomValues() or crypto.randomUUID() for security-sensitive operations.
Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins.

bugs.message: NaN comparison will always be false.
bugs.suggestion: Use Number.isNaN() instead of comparing with NaN.

bugs.message: Array.sort() without compare function may produce unexpected results.
bugs.suggestion: Provide a compare function for consistent sorting.

vulnerabilities.message: Force unwrap detected. May cause runtime crash if value is nil.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection.

vulnerabilities.message: Unsafe deserialization detected.

vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize command arguments. Avoid string interpolation for commands.

vulnerabilities.suggestion: Encode user input before rendering in HTML responses.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and canonicalize file paths before use.

vulnerabilities.message: Weak hashing algorithm detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or stronger for security-sensitive operations.

bugs.message: Potential null pointer or unsafe cast.

vulnerabilities.message: SOQL/SOSL injection via string concatenation.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use bind variables in SOQL queries: [SELECT Id FROM Account WHERE Name = :nameVar].

vulnerabilities.message: Class missing 'with sharing' keyword. DML may bypass record-level security.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Add 'with sharing' to enforce record-level security, or document why 'without sharing' is needed.

vulnerabilities.message: Hard-coded Salesforce ID detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Custom Settings, Custom Metadata, or queries to retrieve IDs dynamically.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential XSS via URL parameters in Visualforce.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use HTMLENCODE(), JSENCODE(), or URLENCODE() for user input in output.

vulnerabilities.message: DML operation inside a loop. May hit governor limits.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Collect records in a list and perform DML after the loop.

vulnerabilities.message: SOQL query inside a loop. May hit governor limits.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Query records before the loop or use a Map for lookups.

vulnerabilities.message: Possible hard-coded credentials detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Named Credentials or Custom Metadata for secrets.

vulnerabilities.message: Error message may leak sensitive information.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use generic error messages for user-facing errors.

bugs.message: Potential null pointer dereference or unchecked collection access.
bugs.suggestion: Check for null and verify collection size before accessing elements.

bugs.message: Empty catch block silently swallows errors.
bugs.suggestion: Log the exception or handle it appropriately.

vulnerabilities.message: Unsafe Obj.magic bypasses type system. May cause undefined behavior.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use proper type conversion functions or redesign data flow to avoid type coercion.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command execution detected. May be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize input. Use Unix.execvp with explicit argument arrays.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection via string formatting.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with your database library.

vulnerabilities.message: Unsafe deserialization via Marshal module.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Marshal is not safe for untrusted data. Use a structured format like JSON or Protobuf.

vulnerabilities.message: Possible hard-coded credentials detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use environment variables or a configuration file excluded from version control.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation with string concatenation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and canonicalize file paths before use.

bugs.message: Potential infinite loop or unbounded recursion.
bugs.suggestion: Ensure loop or recursion has a clear termination condition.
This binding has no effect.

vulnerabilities.message: XSS via unescaped expression. User input rendered directly in HTML.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use JSTL <c:out> or fn:escapeXml() to encode output.

vulnerabilities.message: Scriptlet outputs user input without encoding.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use JSTL tags instead of scriptlets. Encode output with ESAPI or fn:escapeXml().

vulnerabilities.message: EL injection or unsafe Expression Language usage.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate input and use proper EL escaping. Consider isELIgnored where appropriate.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL injection via string concatenation in JSP.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use PreparedStatement with parameterized queries.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command injection via request parameter.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Never pass user input directly to system commands.

vulnerabilities.message: Unsafe deserialization of request data.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid deserializing untrusted data. Use JSON or XML with proper validation.

vulnerabilities.message: Hard-coded credentials in JSP.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use JNDI resources or environment variables for sensitive configuration.

vulnerabilities.message: Open redirect vulnerability. Redirect URL from user input.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate redirect URLs against an allowlist.

bugs.message: Instance variables in JSP are shared across requests (thread-unsafe).
bugs.suggestion: Use local variables within scriptlets or request-scoped attributes.
This assignment has no effect.

codeSmells.message: TODO/FIXME comment found.
codeSmells.suggestion: Track this task in your issue tracker.

codeSmells.message: Scriptlet usage in JSP. Prefer JSTL/EL.
codeSmells.suggestion: Replace scriptlets with JSTL tags and Expression Language for better maintainability.

vulnerabilities.message: Possible secret or credential exposed in JSON configuration.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use environment variables, secret managers, or .env files excluded from version control.

vulnerabilities.message: Cloud provider or service credential detected in JSON.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Remove credentials immediately. Use IAM roles, service accounts, or secret managers.

vulnerabilities.message: Database connection string with credentials detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use environment variables or secret managers for connection strings.

codeSmells.message: Debug or development mode enabled in configuration.
codeSmells.suggestion: Ensure debug mode is disabled in production configurations.

codeSmells.message: Security feature may be disabled in configuration.
codeSmells.suggestion: Review security settings and ensure they are appropriate for production.

codeSmells.message: Clear-text HTTP URL in configuration.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use HTTPS for non-local endpoints.

codeSmells.message: Overly permissive configuration value.
codeSmells.suggestion: Restrict to specific values instead of wildcards.

vulnerabilities.message: Hard-coded secret in Terraform configuration.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use variables with sensitive = true, or reference secrets from a vault.

vulnerabilities.message: Security group allows unrestricted inbound access.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Restrict ingress rules to specific CIDR blocks and ports.

vulnerabilities.message: Security group allows unrestricted outbound access.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Restrict egress to necessary ports and destinations.

vulnerabilities.message: Storage resource missing encryption configuration.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Enable encryption at rest for all storage resources.

vulnerabilities.message: Resource configured for public access.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid public access unless explicitly required. Use private subnets.

vulnerabilities.message: Logging not enabled for resource.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Enable logging and monitoring for audit and security purposes.

vulnerabilities.message: S3 bucket versioning not enabled.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Enable versioning for data protection and recovery.

vulnerabilities.message: Security group rule allows all ports.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Restrict to only the ports required by the application.

vulnerabilities.message: IAM policy with overly permissive permissions.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Follow least-privilege principle. Specify explicit actions and resources.

vulnerabilities.message: Weak TLS/SSL policy configured.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use TLS 1.2 or later. Choose a modern security policy.

codeSmells.message: Sensitive variable has a default value.
codeSmells.suggestion: Remove default values for sensitive variables. Require them to be provided at runtime.

codeSmells.message: Module source not pinned to a specific version.
codeSmells.suggestion: Pin module sources to specific tags or commit hashes.

codeSmells.message: TODO/FIXME comment found.
codeSmells.suggestion: Track this task in your issue tracker.

codeSmells.message: Provider version not pinned.
codeSmells.suggestion: Pin provider versions to avoid unexpected breaking changes.

vulnerabilities.message: Container running as root user.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Add a USER instruction to run as a non-root user.

vulnerabilities.message: Using ADD instead of COPY. ADD has extra functionality that may be unexpected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use COPY for local files. Use RUN curl/wget for remote files to control caching.

vulnerabilities.message: Secret exposed in ENV or ARG instruction. Visible in image layers.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Docker secrets, BuildKit --mount=type=secret, or runtime environment variables.

vulnerabilities.message: Base image not pinned to a specific version or digest.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Pin base images to specific versions or SHA digests for reproducible builds.

vulnerabilities.message: Piping remote script to shell. Downloaded code may be tampered with.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Download, verify checksum, then execute in separate steps.

vulnerabilities.message: Exposing remote access port (SSH/RDP/VNC) in container.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid exposing remote access ports. Use docker exec for debugging.

vulnerabilities.message: Overly permissive file permissions in container.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use least-privilege permissions. Avoid 777.

codeSmells.message: Package manager cache not cleaned in same layer.
codeSmells.suggestion: Clean package manager cache in the same RUN instruction to reduce image size.

codeSmells.message: Package install without -y flag or uncontrolled upgrade.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use -y flag and pin package versions for reproducible builds.

codeSmells.message: Copying entire build context. May include unwanted files.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use .dockerignore and copy only necessary files.

codeSmells.message: TODO/FIXME comment found.
codeSmells.suggestion: Track this task in your issue tracker.

codeSmells.message: Using sudo in container. Containers should manage permissions via USER.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use USER instruction instead of sudo. Set up permissions during build.

vulnerabilities.message: Script injection via untrusted GitHub event data.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Store event data in an environment variable first, then reference the variable.

vulnerabilities.message: Secret may be exposed in CI/CD configuration or logs.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use CI/CD secret management. Never echo secrets to logs.

vulnerabilities.message: Action or image not pinned to a specific SHA or immutable version.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Pin actions to full commit SHA and images to digest for supply chain security.

vulnerabilities.message: Overly permissive token permissions.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Follow least-privilege principle. Grant only necessary permissions.

vulnerabilities.message: Potentially dangerous trigger. pull_request_target runs with write access.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Be cautious with pull_request_target. Never checkout untrusted PR code with this trigger.

vulnerabilities.message: Downloading and executing remote scripts in pipeline.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Download, verify integrity with checksums, then execute.

vulnerabilities.message: Secret may be leaked to environment or logs.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Never write secrets to GITHUB_ENV or log output. Use masked outputs.

codeSmells.message: Step configured to continue on error. Failures may be silently ignored.
codeSmells.suggestion: Remove continue-on-error unless explicitly needed and documented.

codeSmells.message: Excessively long timeout configured.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use reasonable timeouts to catch hanging processes early.

codeSmells.message: TODO/FIXME comment found.
codeSmells.suggestion: Track this task in your issue tracker.

codeSmells.message: Step runs regardless of previous step outcome.
codeSmells.suggestion: Consider using more specific conditions to avoid unnecessary runs on failures.

codeSmells.message: Cache configuration may be incomplete.
codeSmells.suggestion: Include a cache key with a hash of lock files for deterministic caching.

vulnerabilities.message: Unsafe eval usage. May execute arbitrary commands.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid eval. Use arrays and proper quoting instead.

vulnerabilities.message: Command injection via variable interpolation in subshell.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Quote all variables and use arrays for command arguments.

vulnerabilities.message: Unquoted variable. May cause word splitting and globbing.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Always double-quote variables: "$var" instead of $var.

vulnerabilities.message: Dangerous rm command with variable path. May delete unintended files.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate paths before deletion. Use rm with --preserve-root.

vulnerabilities.message: Downloading and piping to shell, or skipping TLS verification.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Download to a file, verify checksum, then execute.

vulnerabilities.message: Possible hard-coded credentials or secret exposure in script.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use environment variables from a secure source. Never echo secrets.

vulnerabilities.message: Insecure temporary file creation. May be vulnerable to symlink attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use mktemp (without -u) to create temporary files securely.

bugs.message: Unquoted variable in test/comparison. May cause syntax errors with empty values.
bugs.suggestion: Quote variables in test expressions: [ "$var" = "value" ].

bugs.message: Piping to while loop creates a subshell. Variables set inside will be lost.
bugs.suggestion: Use process substitution: while read line; do ... done < <(command).
Use = for POSIX compatibility or use [[ ]] for bash-specific features.

codeSmells.message: Script may be missing set -euo pipefail for strict error handling.
codeSmells.suggestion: Add 'set -euo pipefail' after the shebang for safer script execution.
